root_text.sn55.44:1.1: “Catūhi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako ‘aḍḍho mahaddhano mahābhogo’ti vuccati.
root_text.sn55.44:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.44:2.3: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti;
root_text.sn55.44:2.6: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.44:2.7: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako ‘aḍḍho mahaddhano mahābhogo’ti vuccatī”ti.

translation_text.sn55.44:1.1: “Mendicants, a noble disciple who has four things is said to be rich, affluent, and wealthy.
translation_text.sn55.44:2.2: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.44:2.6: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.44:2.7: A noble disciple who has these four things is said to be rich, affluent, and wealthy.”

variant_text.sn55.44:1.1: mahaddhano → mahaḍḍhano (bj) | mahābhogo’ti → mahābhogo mahāyasoti (sya-all, pts1ed, mr)
